articles.title: Zenzizenzizenzic
articles.summary: Zenzizenzizenzic is an obsolete mathematical notation representing the eighth power of a number, dating back to a time when powers were written out in words rather than as superscript numbers. This term was suggested by Robert Recorde, a 16th-century Welsh physician and mathematician, who used it to express the square of squares squaredly. The word has more Zs than any other word in the Oxford English Dictionary.

articles.title: Surprising Economics of Load-Balanced Systems
articles.summary: The M/M/c model may not behave like you expect due to an infinite queue behind a load balancer with no internal queuing, leading to linear improvements in offered load and constant client-observed mean request time. This is because the system can handle double the load without queuing at half load, but only 3.6% of traffic will experience additional latency. The correct answer to the Twitter poll is that the mean latency decreases quickly approaching one second as c increases.
